title: Navigation Redesign
pitch: Analytics showed customers were overwhelmingly clicking primary categories and largely ignoring the subcategory depth the mega menus maintained. On desktop, mega menus were restructured into a consistent 4-column layout. On mobile, the nested drill-down was replaced with a tabbed interface: horizontal audience tabs at the top, collapsible sections within each tab, and a persistent utility footer, all in a single scrollable viewport.
problem: The navigation had been built up over time to accommodate an expanding catalog, but the data told a different story. Customers clicked primary categories and ignored the subcategory depth. On mobile, the multi-level drill-down required too many taps to reach what should have been a one-tap destination.
hypothesis: Simplifying the navigation around actual customer behavior, reducing depth on desktop and replacing drill-downs with tabs on mobile, would improve category discovery and reduce navigation abandonment.
